anchors.operator_authorization_phrase_p2: Authorize s12 D1 MNQ.c.0 P2 phase-2 plan only. Preserve REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure as binding.

c1_live_mode_refusal.description: Algorithm refuses to run in live mode at Initialize. Output JSONs carry explicit trading and live status markers.
must_not: ever conditionally bypass the LiveMode check
reduce the status strings to anything weaker than PAUSED / BLOCKED_AT_6_GATES
c1_live_mode_refusal.required_initialize_block: if self.LiveMode: raise Exception("LIVE_PATH_DETECTED: s12-d1-mnq-c0-single-instrument-donchian-15-8 is paper-only forever; refuse to run in live mode.")

c2_provenance_contract.description: Every backtest/simulator output tagged with deterministic engine-truth run_id from sealed inheritance chain + algorithm version + actual run window.
must_not: hash run_id from operator-mutable fields
skip the Initialize date cross-check
use CONFIG dates for run_id or CAGR when engine dates are readable
allow run_id to come from a hardcoded literal

required_config_fields.plan_doc_sha256: (populated at P3 BUILD; sha256 of the P1 plan-lock MD file on disk)

c2_provenance_contract.required_output_seal_method: LESSON_HUNTER_004 canonical roundtrip (sha256 of json.dumps sort_keys=True separators=',:' ensure_ascii=False default=str EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method)

asset_class_conditional_counters_s12_d1_.atr_stop_breach_count: NEW s12-d1-specific (disclosure; tracks count of stop-loss exits as fraction of total exits for whipsaw signature)
asset_class_conditional_counters_s12_d1_.donchian_15_8_signal_count_per_year: NEW s12-d1-specific (disclosure; tracks per-year trade density to inform DR10 risk monitoring)
asset_class_conditional_counters_s12_d1_.expiry_or_settlement_fills_outside_rth_c: NOT_APPLICABLE (continuous front-month per Databento stype_in=continuous; vendor-side roll; no expiry surface)
asset_class_conditional_counters_s12_d1_.extended_hours_fill_warning_count: NOT_APPLICABLE (single-instrument futures; MNQ session structure does NOT have extended-hours-as-violation semantics that equity options have)
asset_class_conditional_counters_s12_d1_.unsupported_order_type_detected_count: NOT_APPLICABLE (s12-d1 uses CSV-based simulator at P6; QC live brokerage path is structurally not in this candidate's chain)

must_not: conflate QC lifecycle events with operator violations
mutate a counter without a corresponding _forbidden_action_attempts or _skip_log entry
use per-minute increments when per-day is intended

all_safety_warnings_zero.required: True iff all applicable counters are 0

c4_rth_execution_discipline.boundary_alignment_rule_s12_d1: trivially satisfied (eod_cancel_time == rth_safe_window_close == [16, 0]); both reduce to daily-bar close for s12-d1's daily-only design
c4_rth_execution_discipline.limit_order_discipline_s12_d1: NOT_APPLICABLE at P6 simulator level (CSV-based simulator transacts at daily close; LIMIT-order discipline applies at hypothetical future P3.7+ QC live-path which is BLOCKED by 6-gate live-block invariant)
must_not: set eod_cancel_time later than rth_safe_window_close (trivially satisfied for daily-bar design)
submit MarketOrder/MarketOnClose for opens (NOT APPLICABLE at P6 CSV-simulator)
use a wider RTH safe window than the asset's regular session (09:30-16:00 ET is MNQ regular session)

required_config_for_s12_d1.max_quote_age_seconds: NOT_APPLICABLE (daily bars; no intraday quote-age check needed)
required_config_for_s12_d1.max_relative_spread: NOT_APPLICABLE (daily bars; no intraday spread check needed)

c4_rth_execution_discipline.required_eod_cleanup_s12_d1: NOT_APPLICABLE (daily bars; no intraday pending orders; CSV-based simulator at P6 handles fills atomically per daily bar)
required_per_candidate_checks_in_chain_l: Daily bar at RTH close (16:00 ET America/New_York); intraday checks NOT APPLICABLE
bid/ask checks NOT APPLICABLE for daily-bar OHLCV ingestion
Spread checks NOT APPLICABLE for daily-bar OHLCV ingestion

must_not: introduce a synthetic corporate-action list to apply equity-class C5 logic to futures (NOT APPLICABLE)
submit MarketOnClose for the underlying contract (NOT APPLICABLE at P6 CSV-simulator level)
modify the Databento continuous-stitch method post-seal
operator_responsibilities_for_s12_d1: Verify the sealed MNQ.c.0 CSV at sha 8b7b832c...fa23e remains byte-stable on disk at every phase
No fresh fetch authorized; no re-stitching authorized; no roll-override authorized
c5_corporate_action_event_risk_contract.rationale: MNQ.c.0 is a continuous front-month futures contract synthesized vendor-side by Databento (stype_in=continuous). Futures do NOT have stock splits, dividends, or option-style corporate actions. Quarterly contract roll is handled by the Databento continuous-stitch (documented in s10-D1 audit; sealed CSV at sha 8b7b832c...fa23e). The C5 corporate-action blackout / known_corporate_actions / LIMIT pre-close / MOC-rejection-detection mechanisms are STRUCTURALLY ABSENT for this candidate.

required_config_for_s12_d1.futures_roll_method: Databento native continuous-front-month synthesis (stype_in=continuous); vendor-side; documented byte-equivalent from s11-d1 v1

must_not: emit win_rate as a number when closed_trades < verdict_min_closed_trades
compute CAGR from CONFIG dates
omit report_seal_sha256
silently bump schema_id
omit REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure from inherited_constraints_block
performance_summary_required_fields: max_drawdown_pct (magnitude-based; per s11-d1 rev2 condition (e) correction byte-equivalent)
c6_diagnostic_output_schema.rec1_oos_k9_risk_disclosure_in_inherited: OOS K9 EXPECTED TO FIRE. Implied OOS trade count over 2.0y at IS rate is approximately 35-87 trades, below K9 = 100. If OOS K9 fires, the OOS verdict will be OOS_INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE or PARKED_SAFE_BUT_OOS_INDETERMINATE analogous to S10-D2 P11 PARK at 23c7164. The chain shall NOT relax K9 at OOS; the appropriate response is to seal the INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE / INDETERMINATE verdict and park the candidate. Pursuing s12-d1 accepts the structural likelihood of an OOS PARK outcome.
required_top_level_fields_universal: title (must mention paper-research-only + diagnostic-only + secondary label)
inherited_constraints_block (list of verbatim constraint strings; includes REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure)
negative_invariants (dict; all False except backtest_run=True, schedule_created=True)

must_not: add a new verdict outside the closed enum without an explicit operator-authorized schema bump
interpret READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST as live-ready in any downstream automation
emit numeric win-rate when n < threshold
treat REC1 OOS K9 disclosure as advisory only -- it is binding per operator authorization
c7_verdict_semantics.never_live_clause_LOAD_BEARING: READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST is exclusively a research label. It NEVER signals readiness for live trading. The 6-gate live-block applies regardless of verdict for any candidate inheriting this template, including s12-d1.
c7_verdict_semantics.rec1_explicit_at_c7: Per REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure carried byte-equivalent from SEAL: the EXPECTED OOS verdict for s12-d1 is INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE / OOS_INDETERMINATE. READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST at IS does NOT imply OOS will reach READY; OOS is structurally expected to PARK at K9.

required_config_thresholds_s12_d1.verdict_min_closed_trades_lock_origin: K9 byte-equivalent; LOCKED non-negotiable per no_k9_relaxation

verdict_precedence_high_to_low: 1. unsupported_order_type_detected_count > 0 -> FAIL_SAFETY (top priority; NOT APPLICABLE for s12-d1)
2. stale_fill_warning_count > 0 -> FAIL_SAFETY (extended_hours_fill_warning_count NOT APPLICABLE for s12-d1)
4. else -> READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST (with caveats appended to verdict_reasons if n < 100, DD > concern, etc.)

allowed_operational_statuses.ARCHIVED: Operator action; record preserved, no further activity
allowed_operational_statuses.PARKED_FAILED_SAFETY: Any safety counter > 0 that operator chooses not to repair
allowed_operational_statuses.PARKED_FALSIFIED_AT_OOS: OOS run definitively negative per pre-committed thresholds
allowed_operational_statuses.PARKED_SAFE_BUT_OOS_INDETERMINATE_K9_FIR: (s12-d1 EXPECTED terminal verdict per REC1; analogous to S10-D2 P11 PARK at 23c7164)
allowed_operational_statuses.READY_FOR_LONGER_BACKTEST: After a clean Step 5.x with sufficient sample (>=K9)

must_not: auto-revive a parked candidate
modify a parked candidate's record to claim better economics
use parking as a soft path back to live promotion
interpret REC1 OOS K9 disclosure as advisory only -- it is binding
permanent_attributes_unchanged_by_parkin: REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure (binding; carried into any future PARK memo for s12-d1)
c8_candidate_lifecycle.rec1_explicit_at_c8: s12-d1 expected terminal verdict is PARKED_SAFE_BUT_OOS_INDETERMINATE_K9_FIRED per REC1 oos_k9_risk_disclosure. If OOS K9 fires (expected: 35-87 trades < K9=100), the operator MUST park s12-d1 under that status; the chain shall NOT relax K9 to extract a different verdict.

transitions.revival_from_PARKED: requires FRESH SEALED RESEARCH CYCLE; fresh seals throughout; new plan-lock; new ladder; not a continuation; old run record stays intact
transitions.to_any_PARKED: operator authorization required; writes canonical parking report at reports/external_research_hunter/<candidate>_PARKING_REPORT.md; algorithm source preserved verbatim as reference

c8_candidate_lifecycle.weak_performance_rejection_rule_LOAD_BEA: If a candidate shows decisively negative economics on a meaningful sample (Sharpe < 0, expectancy <= 0, profit-loss ratio < 0.5, closed_trades >= verdict_min_closed_trades), the operator MUST park it. Do NOT iterate parameters in search of better numbers -- that is optimization, which is forbidden by the safety-contract template.
